---
title: "Prospect Theory Biases"
listing:
  contents: "."
  type: grid
  sort: "title asc"
  filter-ui: true
  include:
    categories: "{prospect-theory}"
  fields: [title, description]
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

Kahneman & Tversky's [Prospect Theory](https://web.mit.edu/curhan/www/docs/Articles/15341_Readings/Behavioral_Decision_Theory/Kahneman_Tversky_1979_Prospect_theory.pdf) overturned classical expected utility theory by showing that people evaluate outcomes relative to a reference point, and feel losses roughly twice as acutely as equivalent gains.

![The pain of a loss hurts twice as much as the happiness of a win.](../images/loss-aversion.png){width="600px" fig-align="center"}
